news.title: President of the Treasury Board appearance before the Standing Committee on Government Operations and Estimates (OGGO) – Federal Government Consulting Contracts Awarded to McKinsey & Company – February 8, 2023 - Canada.ca
news.summary: Treasury Board President appeared before parliamentary committee regarding federal consulting contracts awarded to McKinsey, discussing procurement directive and Code of Conduct requirements.

briefing_memo: McKinsey & Company Canada has received $10,000 in federal funding to date, with an additional $3.57 million in Alberta sole-source contracts. A B.C. court certified a Canada-wide class action in January 2023 alleging McKinsey was the "hand in the glove" for improper opioid promotion across Canada, and the Auditor General reported in September 2023 that federal agencies showed frequent disregard of procurement policies when awarding 97 contracts worth $209 million to McKinsey, with 70% non-competitive. McKinsey entered a five-year Deferred Prosecution Agreement with U.S. authorities on December 13, 2024, paying $650 million and committing to comprehensive compliance reforms including risk evaluation processes, quality review programs, and annual compliance certifications with Justice Department and HHS oversight. Given the very recent DPA and ongoing Canadian class action litigation, recommend suspending new federal contracts pending completion of the five-year monitorship and resolution of the certified class action.

actions.title: Conduct enhanced due diligence on procurement compliance and conflict-of-interest safeguards
actions.rationale: Auditor General found federal departments failed to follow procurement rules when awarding McKinsey contracts, indicating systemic contracting irregularities requiring verification of current controls

actions.title: Review terms of US Deferred Prosecution Agreement for relevance to Canadian operations
actions.rationale: McKinsey entered $650M settlement and DPA with US authorities in December 2024 for opioid-related conduct; monitor compliance obligations and assess whether Canadian entity faces ongoing litigation risk

actions.title: Confirm status of certified Canadian class-action lawsuit and potential Crown liability exposure
actions.rationale: B.C. court certified Canada-wide class action against McKinsey for alleged opioid promotion in 2023; federal departments must assess whether contracts create indirect Crown exposure or reputational risk
